prompt_replacements.old: Use reference_value when the input identifies one particular resource.
prompt_replacements.new: Use reference_value when the supplied value names or identifies one particular entity or resource, including when qualifying candidates relate to it.

prompt_replacements.old:
After writing answer_population, inspect each EXPLICIT_USER_CONSTRAINT. A predicate_value is an explicit word or phrase that states which property value qualifying candidates must have. This includes adjectival and compound modifiers; copy the user's phrase without inferring catalog field boundaries.
Each predicate_value supplies the membership test or tests whose required property value it states. A compound phrase may supply several tests. Do not turn the answer subject into an input.
prompt_replacements.new:
Use predicate_value when the supplied value describes a property shared by possible candidates and does not name or identify one particular entity or resource. If both definitions appear to apply, use reference_value.
Copy the user's property-value phrase without inferring catalog field boundaries. One compound phrase may supply several membership tests. Do not turn the answer subject into an input.
